# Architecture Orientation

<!-- DRAFT: Generated by /adev-init. Review and refine as the codebase grows. -->

## Overview

Task Management App is a single Next.js monolith deployed in a Docker container. There are no microservices, no external APIs, and no separate backend — the Next.js app serves everything. SQLite provides persistence via a file mounted as a Docker volume.

The project is currently in the **specification phase**. No application code exists yet. This orientation describes the planned architecture as defined in the feature charters.

## Bounded Contexts

The system is organized into four logical bounded contexts, separated by feature-based file structure (not separate services):

| Context | Entities | Module |
|---------|----------|--------|
| **Identity** | User, Account, Session | `src/features/auth/` |
| **Team** | Team, Membership, Invitation | `src/features/teams/` |
| **Board** | Board, List, Label | `src/features/boards/` |
| **Card** | Card, CardLabel, Comment, ActivityEntry | `src/features/boards/` (shared with Board context) |

Cross-context communication uses **domain events** (typed in-process EventEmitter in `src/shared/lib/events.ts`), not direct imports between feature modules.

## Planned Directory Structure

```
src/
  app/                           # Next.js App Router
    api/
      auth/[...nextauth]/        # NextAuth catch-all route
      teams/                     # Team CRUD endpoints
      boards/                    # Board, list, card endpoints
    (auth)/                      # Auth page group (login, register)
    (dashboard)/                 # Authenticated app shell
      boards/[boardId]/          # Board detail page
  features/
    auth/                        # Identity context
      components/                # Login form, register form
      actions/                   # Server actions for auth flows
      queries/                   # Session queries
    teams/                       # Team context
      components/                # Team list, invite modal
      actions/                   # Team CRUD, invitation actions
      queries/                   # Team/membership queries
    boards/                      # Board + Card contexts
      components/                # Board view, list column, card modal
      actions/                   # Board/list/card mutations
      queries/                   # Board data fetching
    notifications/               # Notification infrastructure
      components/                # Notification bell, dropdown
      queries/                   # Notification fetching
  shared/
    lib/
      prisma.ts                  # Prisma client singleton
      auth.ts                    # NextAuth configuration
      events.ts                  # Domain event emitter (typed)
      search.ts                  # SQLite FTS5 utilities
    components/                  # Layout, navigation, shared UI
    test/                        # Test utilities and helpers
prisma/
  schema.prisma                  # Database schema (all contexts)
  migrations/                    # Prisma migration history
```

## Key Data Flows

1. **Authentication:** Browser → NextAuth route → Prisma session → session cookie
2. **Board operations:** Client Component → Server Action → Prisma → domain event → activity log
3. **Real-time updates:** Client Component → React Query polling (3-5s) → Server Component data fetch
4. **Search:** Client query → API route → `prisma.$queryRaw` against FTS5 virtual table
5. **Notifications:** Domain event → notification handler → in-app notification record → polling delivery

## Key Design Patterns

- **Fractional indexing** for list/card ordering (insert between positions without reindexing)
- **Prisma middleware** for automatic activity logging on all mutations
- **React Query** for client-side cache, polling, and optimistic updates
- **DaisyUI** for pre-built component primitives (cards, modals, badges, themes)
- **Server Components** by default; `'use client'` pushed as far down the tree as possible

## Deployment

Single Docker container with SQLite file as a volume mount. A reverse proxy (Nginx or Caddy) handles TLS termination in front of the container. No cloud services, no managed databases — fully self-hosted.
